breaches.details: Data of 6 million customers exfiltrated. The accessed data includes full names, addresses, phone numbers, customer numbers, email addresses, account numbers, date of birth, and passport and driver’s license information. "Personal data from a customer contact system used by Odido,” though “no passwords, call details, or billing information were involved"
breaches.link: Hack at Odido, data of millions of customers in the hands of criminals

breaches.details: Investigators believe the operation focused on siphoning off technical network information that could support long-term intelligence collection, rather than stealing customer records or causing outages that might draw attention.
breaches.link: Singapore spent 11 months evicting suspected telco spies • The Register

breaches.details: Investigators believe the operation focused on siphoning off technical network information that could support long-term intelligence collection, rather than stealing customer records or causing outages that might draw attention.
breaches.link: Singapore spent 11 months evicting suspected telco spies • The Register

breaches.details: Investigators believe the operation focused on siphoning off technical network information that could support long-term intelligence collection, rather than stealing customer records or causing outages that might draw attention.
breaches.link: Singapore spent 11 months evicting suspected telco spies • The Register

breaches.details: Investigators believe the operation focused on siphoning off technical network information that could support long-term intelligence collection, rather than stealing customer records or causing outages that might draw attention.
breaches.link: Singapore spent 11 months evicting suspected telco spies • The Register

breaches.details: Unregistered devices (femtocells) had accessed its internal network and leaked 22,227 subscriber identification numbers, terminal identification numbers, and phone numbers. This resulted in 368 customers losing ₩243MN ($168,000) in small payment scams.
breaches.link: Korean Telcos Vie for Customers After Data Security Breaches - CX Today

breaches.details: Colt breached on August 12, customer data accessed, and document titles on the dark web.
breaches.consequences: Key platforms offline as precaution, customer networks secure.
breaches.link: Telecom Firm Colt Confirms Data Breach as Ransomware Group Auctions Files - SecurityWeek

breaches.details: 6.4 million customers' data compromised. Stolen data includes customers’ contact information and contractual data, their civil status (or company data if the subscriber is a professional), as well as IBANs, or international bank account numbers. [Another source says information including names, phone and SIM card numbers, tariff plan data and Personal Unlocking Key (PUK) codes]
breaches.link: Data breach at French telecom giant Bouygues affects millions of customers | TechCrunch

breaches.details: Detected a cyberattack “on one of its information systems”. No evidence to suggest that any internal or customer data has been exfiltrated.
breaches.consequences: Disruptions to some of the company’s platforms, as well as business customers and some public sector services mostly in France
breaches.link: Telecom giant Orange warns of disruption amid ongoing cyberattack | TechCrunch

breaches.details: At end of July, Orange Belgium detected a cyberattack on one of its IT systems, resulting in unauthorized access to certain data from 850,000 customer accounts. Not connected to Orange Group attack.
breaches.link: Orange Belgium Data Breach Impacts 850,000 Customers - SecurityWeek

breaches.details: Long term breach (evidence over 2 years). Compromised sensitive SIM card data belonging to nearly 27 million users (network data, not personal data - could be used for SIM cloning).
breaches.consequences: See report The rise of AI at SK Telecom
